description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a raised stone wall forming the well's main body. The well is topped with a prominent, ornate wooden roof featuring curved, tiled eaves in a reddish-brown hue, reminiscent of East Asian architectural styles. Supporting the roof are sturdy wooden beams, and a large wooden wheel mechanism is mounted at the center, likely for drawing water. The well is surrounded by a few scattered stone blocks, possibly for stepping or decoration. The interior of the well appears open, with a visible shaft descending into darkness. The overall construction suggests durability and a blend of functional and aesthetic design.
description: A circular stone water well with a decorative, tiled wooden roof and a central wooden wheel mechanism. The structure combines robust stonework with ornate wooden elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden wheel mechanism and the detailed tiled roof, with the stone well base and a few stone blocks in the foreground.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curved, tiled roof and the open well shaft, with the stone wall and scattered blocks more visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the back of the wooden wheel and the roof's structure, with fewer stone blocks visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the tiled roof, open well shaft, and the stone base, with a single stone block in the foreground.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Large wooden wheel mechanism mounted at the center, likely for drawing water; no visible bucket or rope.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately 1 meter high and a wooden roof canopy supported by beams.
attribute_decorative_elements: Curved tiled roof with ornamental eaves; no additional decorative carvings or embellishments.
